spec_note: No spec authored. After v2 restructure (added §05 Factory internals + §06 Factory layout), spec backfill remains the right next step.
audit_version: v3 (post v2 restructure: factory internals + factory layout added; capex / unit-econ / decision-ask / sources removed; lightbox re-introduced)
v2_to_v3_delta: v2 was 8 sections + hero. v3 is 10 sections + hero. Added §05 Factory internals (6 cards · body-measurement native SVG diagram + Pattern · Cutting · Sewing · Finishing · Folding cards · 3 product photos from deck). Added §06 Factory layout (native floor-plan diagram, 4 zones + capacity/footprint/conveyor callouts). Renumbered Live Kitchen → §07, Operating model → §08, Outcome targets → §09, Phase-gate → §10. Lightbox infrastructure re-introduced (3 zoomable photos now bind).

findings.issue: Per-zone floor percentages (Zone A: ~12% · Zone B: ~38% · Zone B: ~30% · Zone D: ~20%) are fabricated. Concept note (line 140) describes zone *positions* — 'cutting through the centre line · sewing block on the right · finishing/pressing/packing on the south wall · fabric and trims store flush against the entrance' — but assigns no floor percentages. Deck slide 14 'Factory Area Workings' is an EMF (could not read content) — may or may not contain the actual breakdown. Either way, the percentages on the page are not currently sourced from any artefact I can verify.
findings.fix: Two options: (a) drop the four '~XX% of floor' annotations from §06 — the position language alone (centre line / right block / south wall / entrance) carries the spatial story; (b) convert deck slide 14 (EMF) to PNG/PDF, read the actual area workings, and replace the fabricated numbers with sourced ones. Option (a) is faster and honest; option (b) preserves the visual but requires source-side conversion.

findings.section: §05 Factory internals · Card 3 Cutting + §06 Factory layout · Zone B
findings.issue: Page uses 'Morgan Tecnica' but both source documents (concept note rows 5-7 capex table and deck slide 7) consistently say 'Morgan Italia'. The product photo on Card 3 actually shows 'MorganTecnica' branding on the cutter, suggesting the company name is Morgan Tecnica srl (real Italian manufacturer in Carpi) and that 'Morgan Italia' is likely a colloquial reference to the same vendor. Page → source mismatch either way.
findings.fix: Change 'Morgan Tecnica' → 'Morgan Italia' across §05 Card 3 caption, §05 Card 3 machinery line, and §06 Zone B equipment list. Three occurrences. Or, if Morgan Tecnica is the verified brand name, update the SOURCE documents instead — but the page should never drift from source unilaterally.

findings.issue: Section describes the factory floor plan in present-tense detail (zones, equipment, conveyor) without a status pill. The factory does not exist; the layout is a design proposal. tone-of-voice / audit-framework rule: every claim that needs a status pill carries one. Hero pills + §02 Status block establish that the project is proposal-stage, but a reader landing directly on §06 from a deep-link or scroll could read it as describing a built thing.
findings.fix: Add a 'Proposal' pill to the §06 H2 row (or as the first element of the layout card header). Single-line fix.

findings.issue: Same finding as v2. Page structure deviates from canonical default ordering without a recorded spec entry. v3 deepened the deviation (added §05 Factory internals + §06 Factory layout — both are deep-dive sections that don't map cleanly to canonical §03 Architecture / §04 Deep dive).
findings.fix: Backfill docs/dark-factory-spec.md with §3 page-structure recording all overrides as D-entries in §9 Decisions. Process gap, not a page-quality issue, but worth doing for future revisions.

findings.issue: Native body silhouette renders but the figure has a stick-figure quality (the arm and leg paths are simple polylines). 14 measurement dots position approximately rather than anatomically. Acceptable as a stylized representation but not as polished as the equipment photos in cards 3, 4, 6.
findings.fix: Either (a) accept as-is — it's a stylized diagram, not a clinical anatomy chart; or (b) replace with a tighter SVG silhouette (use a more curved body path, position dots at anatomical reference points). Lower priority — the diagram communicates 'body scan + 14 measurements' clearly.

findings.issue: Zone naming convention 'Zone A / B / C / D' is the page's own labeling for navigation — not in the source. Acceptable as a presentation device but worth noting that the labels are page-introduced.
findings.fix: Keep as-is — the labels are a useful reading aid, and the alphabetic ordering follows the entrance → exit flow direction. No action.

findings.issue: Card 1 H3 reads 'Phone-camera scan + Bodygram + IR fit engines.' — adequate, but the chain of three nouns separated by '+' could read as a stack-list rather than a sentence. Card lede formula prefers '{Function} for {audience}. {Capability}. {Number}.' format.
findings.fix: Optional rewrite: 'Phone scan in 30 seconds. Bodygram + IR fit engines translate to 14 measurements with mean error < 1 cm.' — leads with the action and the numbers.

cross_page.mega_menu_consistency: PASS · all sibling pages identical · zero drift

strengths: Factory internals depth is now first-class. v3 added six equipment-detail cards (Body measurement → Pattern → Cutting → Sewing → Finishing → Folding) sourced from the deck I had missed in v1/v2. Body-measurement card carries a native SVG silhouette with 14 measurement dots; cards 3, 4, 6 carry product photos of Morgan Tecnica/Italia cutter, Juki sewing automat, EPA-5001 folding line. Brand names exact across the line.

§06 Factory layout is a native diagram in the design language — Zone A entrance band, Zone B Cutting + Zone C Sewing in a 7/5 grid, Zone D south-wall band, plus three annotation cards (Eton conveyor / Capacity / Footprint). Reads as a floor plan, not a card grid.
Visual evidence baseline rises from 'card text only' (v1) to 'cards + photos + 2 native diagrams + 1 native flow + 1 native timeline'. Lightbox infrastructure correctly bound to the 3 product photos.
Honesty register stays calibrated. One Live pill (Horizon, correctly), all other status claims framed as Proposal / Pending / Roadmap. Zero overclaims even in the new factory-internals depth.

Tone-of-voice is exceptionally clean across the 10 sections: zero banned vocab matches across 26+ terms; zero 'we / our'; zero exclamations or emojis; AJIO and Reliance Trends casing exact; hero subhead 29 words within ceiling. The H1 / M1 / L2 findings from v2 audit all carried through to v3.
